title: Define application/bolyra-proof+cbor content type and envelope schema

artifacts.description: CDDL schema covering human-proof, agent-proof, and delegation-proof envelope types with public signals, session nonce binding, and discriminant tag
IANA considerations section addendum: media type registration template for application/bolyra-proof+cbor per RFC 6838 structured syntax suffix rules
Reference CBOR encoder/decoder for ProofEnvelope using cbor-x; exports encodeProofEnvelope() and decodeProofEnvelope() with full TypeScript types
TypeScript type definitions for ProofEnvelope, HumanProofPayload, AgentProofPayload, DelegationProofPayload, and SessionNonceBinding
Round-trip encode/decode tests for all three proof types, nonce binding verification, unknown field rejection, and malformed input error handling
Developer-facing guide: content-type negotiation flow, HTTP header examples, MCP tool payload shape, and framework-agnostic deserialization pattern

steps: Audit existing proof output shapes in sdk/src/ (proveHandshake, createAgentCredential, delegation stub) to extract all public signal field names and types
Define CDDL top-level discriminant: proof-envelope = human-proof-envelope / agent-proof-envelope / delegation-proof-envelope with a uint version tag (major type 0, value 1)
Write CDDL for human-proof-envelope: { version: 1, type: 0, humanMerkleRoot: bstr .size 32, nullifierHash: bstr .size 32, nonceBinding: bstr .size 32, groth16Proof: groth16-proof }

Write CDDL for agent-proof-envelope: { version: 1, type: 1, credentialCommitment: bstr .size 32, permissionBits: uint .size 1, expiryEpoch: uint, nonceBinding: bstr .size 32, provingSystem: proving-system, proof: groth16-proof / plonk-proof }

Write CDDL for delegation-proof-envelope: { version: 1, type: 2, parentCredentialHash: bstr .size 32, delegatedPermissionBits: uint .size 1, delegateePubKey: bstr .size 32, nonceBinding: bstr .size 32, provingSystem: proving-system, proof: groth16-proof / plonk-proof }
Define shared CDDL types: groth16-proof = { pi_a: [bstr, bstr], pi_b: [[bstr, bstr], [bstr, bstr]], pi_c: [bstr, bstr] }; plonk-proof = bstr; proving-system = 0 / 1 (groth16 / plonk)

Add IANA considerations section to spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md: type name 'application', subtype 'bolyra-proof+cbor', required parameters (version), encoding (CBOR per RFC 8949), interoperability considerations, security considerations reference
Create sdk/src/types/envelope.ts with TypeScript interfaces mirroring the CDDL schema; use branded types for 32-byte fixed-length bstr fields

Install cbor-x as a peer dependency in sdk/package.json (already a transitive dep via snarkjs path — confirm before adding); implement encodeProofEnvelope() using cbor-x encode with deterministic option enabled
Implement decodeProofEnvelope() with strict validation: check version tag, discriminant type field, required field presence, and byte-length constraints; throw typed BolyraEnvelopeError on violations
Write SDK tests covering: human/agent/delegation round-trips, nonce binding preserved, wrong version rejected, truncated bytes rejected, extra unknown fields stripped with warning
Wire encodeProofEnvelope/decodeProofEnvelope into proveHandshake return value and verifyHandshake input to make the wire format the canonical interchange surface
Update docs/wire-format.md with HTTP example (Content-Type: application/bolyra-proof+cbor, Accept negotiation), MCP tool payload wrapper pattern, and Node/Python deserialization snippets
Export encodeProofEnvelope and decodeProofEnvelope from sdk/src/index.ts public API surface
dependencies: RFC 6838 — media type structured syntax suffixes (+cbor)
RFC 9090 — CBOR tags registry (for potential future tag allocation)
spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md — existing IETF draft to receive IANA section
sdk/src/index.ts — public API surface that must export new encoder/decoder
circuits/build/*.vkey.json — public signal names must match CDDL field definitions exactly
